title: YELP Fellowship 2026 by LéO Africa Institute: Transformative Leadership Programme for Young African Changemakers
summary: The Young and Emerging Leaders Project (YELP) Fellowship 2026, organized by the LéO Africa Institute in partnership with the Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS), is a prestigious leadership development programme designed to equip Africa’s next generation of changemakers with the values, skills, and networks needed to drive meaningful socio-economic transformation across the continent. Now in its seventh year, the YELP Fellowship has grown into one of Africa’s most respected leadership communities, bringing together diverse young professionals—from entrepreneurs and creatives to policy thinkers and activists—who are committed to lives of purpose, impact, and service. This fellowship is not just a training programme; it is a transformative journey that nurtures values-based leadership and builds a lifelong network of influential African leaders. About the YELP Fellowship The Young and Emerging Leaders Project (YELP) is a flagship initiative of the LéO Africa Institute aimed at fostering a new generation of African leaders grounded in ethics, purpose, and a deep understanding of the continent’s socio-political and economic landscape. The programme focuses on: Values-driven leadership development Critical thinking and self-awareness Understanding Africa’s development challenges Building networks of impact-driven leaders Through immersive learning experiences and community engagement, YELP empowers fellows to lead with integrity and contribute meaningfully to their societies. Programme Overview Key Fellowship Details Programme Name: Young and Emerging Leaders Project (YELP) Fellowship 2026 Organizers: LéO Africa Institute Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS) Duration: Multi-phase fellowship with residential seminars Mode: In-person residential seminars Application Status: Open The fellowship is structured to provide deep engagement through curated sessions and collaborative learning experiences. Programme Structure The YELP Fellowship is delivered through three intensive residential seminars, each focusing on a critical dimension of leadership and development. 1. Values, Ethics Philosophy of Leadership This foundational module focuses on: Personal identity and purpose Ethical leadership and integrity Building conviction and clarity in decision-making Participants are guided to reflect deeply on their leadership journey and align their values with their actions. 2. Political Economy Development This module explores: Systems shaping Africa’s development Governance, policy, and economic structures Decision-making within complex socio-political environments Fellows gain a deeper understanding of how structural forces influence opportunities and challenges across the continent. 3. Media, Art Technology This forward-looking module focuses on: The role of storytelling and narratives Influence of media and digital platforms Technology as a tool for social change Participants explore how creative and technological tools can be leveraged to shape public discourse and drive impact. What Makes YELP Unique The YELP Fellowship stands out due to its holistic and values-centered approach to leadership development. Key Highlights Focus on values-based leadership rather than technical skills alone Interdisciplinary learning across politics, economics, media, and culture Strong emphasis on self-awareness and purpose Access to a growing pan-African network Engagement with thought leaders and practitioners Fellowship Community and Network YELP is more than a programme—it is a lifelong community. Alumni Impact Fellows from over 8 African countries Diverse representation across sectors Ongoing collaboration and mentorship opportunities Participants become part of a network of leaders committed to shaping Africa’s future. Who Should Apply The fellowship is open to a wide range of individuals who are committed to leadership and impact. Target Participants Entrepreneurs Media practitioners Social innovators Artists and creatives Activists and community leaders Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be between 21 and 35 years old Be from any African country Be from any professional or creative background Demonstrate commitment to: Personal growth Values-driven leadership Community and continental impact Application Process The application process is conducted online through a structured multi-step form. Application Steps Personal Information Professional Background Reflective Questions Final Submission Applicants are required to provide: Personal details Professional experience Responses to reflective leadership questions Application Deadline: 30th April, 2026. Click HERE to start your application. Why You Should Apply The YELP Fellowship offers a transformative experience for young African leaders. Key Benefits Develop strong leadership identity and purpose Gain insights into Africa’s political and economic systems Build a powerful network of changemakers Enhance communication, critical thinking, and influence Access mentorship and long-term collaboration opportunities Learn more about the Young and Emerging Leaders Project (YELP) Fellowship 2026 HERE. title: KIPPRA Young Professionals Programme 2026/2027: Build a Career in Public Policy Research
summary: The KIPPRA Young Professionals Programme 2026/2027 is a one-year, research-focused training opportunity designed for graduates seeking to build expertise in public policy analysis and development. Hosted by the Kenya Institute for Public Policy Research and Analysis (KIPPRA), this programme equips young professionals with the technical, analytical, and practical skills needed to contribute to national development planning and policy formulation. Targeted at early-career professionals with a strong academic background, the programme combines structured training with hands-on research experience. Participants are placed within specialized research divisions and are expected to produce publishable research outputs by the end of the programme. About the Program The KIPPRA Young Professionals Programme is an annual initiative aimed at strengthening human and institutional capacity in public policy research. It focuses on building competencies in key areas such as public policy processes, research methodologies, governance systems, macroeconomic modelling, and research ethics. Participants are placed in one of several research divisions, including Macroeconomics, Statistics, Predictive Analytics, Governance, Trade and Foreign Policy, and Social Sectors, among others. Each division focuses on a specific thematic area, allowing participants to deepen their expertise while contributing to ongoing policy research. Throughout the year, young professionals engage in applied research, data analysis, and policy evaluation. The programme emphasizes both theoretical knowledge and practical application, ensuring that participants gain a well-rounded understanding of policy development processes within a real-world context. Why This Opportunity Matters Effective public policy is essential for sustainable national development, particularly in rapidly evolving economic and social environments. However, there is often a gap between academic training and the practical skills required to design, implement, and evaluate policies. The KIPPRA Young Professionals Programme addresses this gap by providing early-career professionals with direct exposure to policy research and analysis. It enables participants to work on real policy issues, contribute to evidence-based decision-making, and understand the complexities of governance in a devolved system. This opportunity is particularly important for young professionals in Kenya and those legally residing in the country, as it strengthens local capacity to address national development challenges. By investing in emerging talent, the programme contributes to building a pipeline of skilled policy researchers and analysts. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the programme benefit from an intensive learning experience that combines academic training with practical application. One of the key advantages is the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in policy research, including data collection, analysis, and report writing. The programme also helps participants develop strong analytical and problem-solving skills, which are essential for careers in public policy, research, and development. Working within specialized divisions allows participants to build subject-matter expertise in their chosen field. In addition, participants expand their professional networks by collaborating with experienced researchers, policymakers, and fellow young professionals. The requirement to produce publishable research outputs further enhances their professional profile and opens doors to future academic and career opportunities. Overall, the programme provides a solid foundation for those seeking careers in government, research institutions, international organizations, or the private sector. Who Can Apply The programme is open to individuals who have completed a Master’s degree in a relevant field and are interested in public policy research. Applicants must be below the age of 35 at the time of application. Candidates are expected to demonstrate strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, as well as proficiency in at least one statistical software package such as R, STATA, SPSS, or E-Views. Good communication and interpersonal skills are also essential. Fluency in English is required, and applicants must be legally residing in Kenya at the time of participation. Successful candidates will also need to obtain clearance from several regulatory and oversight institutions as part of the selection process. Eligible Areas This programme is specifically targeted at individuals who are legally living in Kenya. Applicants from diverse backgrounds, including persons with disabilities, women, and individuals from marginalized communities, are strongly encouraged to apply. Application Process and Deadline Applications must be submitted online through the official KIPPRA recruitment portal. Candidates are required to indicate their preferred research division and ensure that all required information is completed accurately. Incomplete applications will not be considered, and hard-copy submissions are not accepted. Public sector applicants must provide endorsement from their respective institutions where applicable. The application deadline is 21 April 2026 at 11:59 PM , and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Learn more on the official KIPPRA website . title: WHO JPO Programme 2026: Paid Global Health Role in Denmark for Young Professionals
summary: The WHO JPO Programme 2026 offers a fully paid international opportunity for early-career public health professionals from eligible developing countries to gain hands-on experience with the World Health Organization. Based in Copenhagen and funded by the Government of the Netherlands, this Junior Professional Officer (JPO) role focuses on advancing sexual, reproductive, maternal, and newborn health across the WHO European Region. This opportunity is designed for young professionals who are ready to contribute to global health systems while building a long-term career in international development. With structured training, mentorship, and exposure to high-level policy work, the programme provides a strong foundation for those seeking to work within the United Nations system. About the Program The WHO JPO Programme is part of a broader initiative to strengthen global health leadership by equipping early-career professionals with practical experience in international organizations. This specific position sits within the Division of Health Systems at WHO, which supports countries in building resilient, people-centred healthcare systems. As a JPO, you will work on issues related to sexual and reproductive health and rights, maternal and newborn care, and adolescent health. The role includes contributing to policy development, supporting country-level implementation, and collaborating with global partners such as UNICEF, UNFPA, and the World Bank. The programme also includes a structured learning component. Participants gain insight into WHO’s operations at regional and global levels while developing technical, analytical, and leadership skills. A dedicated training and travel allowance further supports professional development throughout the assignment. Why This Opportunity Matters Global health systems continue to face significant challenges, particularly in ensuring equitable access to quality care for women, children, and vulnerable populations. Addressing gaps in maternal and newborn health, as well as advancing sexual and reproductive health rights, remains a priority for many countries. This opportunity is important because it places young professionals at the centre of these efforts. By working directly with WHO and its partners, participants contribute to real-world solutions that improve health outcomes and strengthen systems at scale. At the same time, the programme helps bridge the gap between academic training and practical experience. It enables emerging professionals from developing countries to access global platforms, contribute to international policy discussions, and bring valuable perspectives from their home contexts. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the WHO JPO Programme benefit from a comprehensive professional development experience. Beyond gaining technical knowledge, they develop the skills and networks needed to succeed in global health careers. The role offers direct exposure to international health policy and systems strengthening, allowing participants to work on real projects that impact multiple countries. You will build strong analytical and research skills through data collection, policy reviews, and reporting tasks. In addition, the programme provides opportunities to collaborate with leading international organizations and experts, expanding your professional network. The structured supervision and mentorship ensure continuous learning, while the annual training allowance supports further skill development. Overall, the experience positions participants for future roles within the United Nations system or other global health institutions. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to early-career professionals who meet specific academic and professional requirements. Applicants should have a background in public health, medicine, or a related field, along with relevant experience in maternal, newborn, or reproductive health. Candidates must demonstrate strong analytical skills, the ability to work in multicultural environments, and a commitment to advancing health equity. Fluency in English is required, and knowledge of another WHO official language is considered an advantage. Applicants should also be under the age of 32 at the time of application and must not have extensive prior experience within the UN system. Eligible Areas This JPO position is exclusively open to nationals from selected developing countries under the Netherlands-sponsored programme. Eligible countries include Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Chad, Colombia, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Ghana, India, Indonesia, Iraq, Jordan, Kenya, Lebanon, Mali, Mozambique, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Tunisia, Uganda, Ukraine, Vietnam, Yemen, and the State of Palestine. Applicants must have completed a substantial part of their academic training in one of these countries. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must submit an online application through the official UNDP or WHO careers portal. Required documents include a motivation letter, academic credentials, and proof of nationality. Shortlisted candidates may be invited to further assessments or interviews as part of the selection process. The application deadline is 5 May 2026 , and interested applicants are encouraged to apply early to ensure all required documents are submitted on time. Learn more and apply on the official job posting . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? title: Pankaj Award 2026: Global Youth Peacebuilding Fund for Leaders Ending Conflict
summary: The Pankaj Award to End Conflict 2026 is a global youth peacebuilding funding opportunity supporting young leaders who are actively working to prevent, reduce, or resolve conflict in their communities and countries. Launched by Regent Global in partnership with One Young World, this initiative provides a total fund of $250,000 to accelerate the impact of emerging peacebuilders aged 18–35 who are addressing the root causes of violence, division, and inequality. This opportunity is designed for young leaders who are building non-profits, social enterprises, or purpose-driven initiatives focused on dialogue, reconciliation, human rights, and sustainable peace. It combines significant financial support with global visibility and access to one of the world’s leading youth leadership networks. About the Program The Pankaj Award to End Conflict was founded by Professor Selva Pankaj and Dr Tharshiny Pankaj, who arrived in the United Kingdom as refugees fleeing the Sri Lankan Civil War. Their lived experience of conflict and displacement deeply shaped their commitment to education, peacebuilding, and youth empowerment. Through Regent Global, they have developed a 25-year legacy of supporting educational innovation. The award represents a continuation of this mission, created to identify and uplift young leaders who are actively working to rebuild trust, reduce violence, and strengthen unity across divided communities. In partnership with One Young World, the award forms part of a broader global movement to support youth leadership in addressing systemic challenges. The program will select ten finalists from around the world, each demonstrating measurable impact in peacebuilding and conflict prevention. Why This Opportunity Matters Conflict continues to affect millions of people globally, often disproportionately impacting marginalised communities, youth, and vulnerable populations. While traditional peacebuilding efforts have played an important role, there is increasing recognition that young leaders are essential in addressing the root causes of violence through innovation, community engagement, and lived experience. The Pankaj Award is significant because it shifts resources directly to those already working on the ground to prevent conflict. It recognises that peacebuilding is not only about post-conflict recovery, but also about prevention, education, and long-term structural change. By investing in young leaders, the award strengthens initiatives that promote dialogue, challenge inequality, empower marginalised groups, and build systems that reduce the likelihood of future conflict. It also provides access to a global platform where ideas, partnerships, and strategies can be shared across regions. What Participants Will Gain Selected finalists of the Pankaj Award will receive a comprehensive package of funding, leadership development, and global exposure designed to scale their impact. Key benefits include: Funding between $10,000 and $100,000 for selected finalists to scale peacebuilding initiatives A fully-funded place at the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa Access to a global network of over 20,000 One Young World Ambassadors across 190+ countries Opportunities for collaboration with international peacebuilders and changemakers Long-term visibility and recognition for conflict prevention work In addition, one winner will be selected to receive the top award of $100,000 to further expand their initiative and deepen their impact. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to young leaders who meet the following criteria: Applicants must: Be aged between 18 and 35 years old Be leading a registered non-profit organisation, social enterprise, or purpose-driven company Hold decision-making authority within their organisation Be actively working on peacebuilding, conflict prevention, or reconciliation initiatives Eligible applicants are typically individuals working on issues such as community dialogue, violence prevention, human rights protection, education for peace, and empowerment of marginalised groups. Application Process and Deadline Applications open on 11 April 2026 and close on 15 June 2026 . Applicants are required to submit details of their organisation and demonstrate evidence of impact in preventing or resolving conflict. The selection process includes review by an independent expert panel, which will assess candidates based on their track record of impact, ability to scale their initiatives, collaboration with affected communities, and long-term sustainability of their solutions. Finalists will be announced following the review period and will participate in the One Young World Summit in November 2026, where the final award recipient will be selected. To learn more and apply, visit the offical One Young World website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Tipping Point Pacific Grant 2026: €10K Feminist Climate Justice Funding for Youth & Indigenous Groups
summary: The Tipping Point Pacific Grant 2026 is a feminist climate justice funding opportunity supporting youth-led, women-led, disability-inclusive, and gender-diverse grassroots organisations in the Pacific working to address climate change, inequality, and environmental injustice. This opportunity provides up to €10,000 in flexible funding for Indigenous and community-rooted groups that are leading local responses to ecological and social challenges across the region. It is designed to strengthen grassroots initiatives that are often excluded from mainstream funding systems while advancing climate justice, gender equality, and Indigenous sovereignty. This grant is particularly relevant for emerging collectives, informal networks, and small organisations working in Pacific Island countries and territories who are building solutions grounded in community knowledge, care, and cultural continuity. About the Program The Tipping Point Pacific Grant is a decolonial feminist funding initiative grounded in the Pacific concept of Vā , which represents the sacred relationships between people, land, and ocean. The program supports grassroots movements that are defending these relationships in the face of climate, social, and economic injustice. Implemented with Oxfam Pacific, the grant prioritises youth-led, women-led, disability-inclusive, and gender-diverse initiatives. It focuses on strengthening locally rooted action in areas such as climate justice, Indigenous knowledge systems, anti-extractive resistance, demilitarisation, storytelling, and community healing. This initiative specifically targets groups that are often excluded from mainstream funding systems, including informal networks and emerging collectives, offering them flexible support to grow their impact. Why This Opportunity Matters The Pacific is one of the regions most affected by climate change, facing rising sea levels, environmental degradation, and increasing social vulnerability. At the same time, it is a hub of powerful Indigenous knowledge, cultural resilience, and community-led climate solutions. Despite their leadership, many grassroots organisations remain underfunded and lack access to global funding systems. The Tipping Point Pacific Grant addresses this gap by shifting resources directly to those leading on the ground. This funding recognises that climate justice is inseparable from gender justice, Indigenous sovereignty, and community wellbeing. By investing in grassroots leadership, the program strengthens long-term resilience and ensures that solutions are shaped by lived experience rather than external systems. What Participants Will Gain Selected groups will benefit from both financial and capacity-building support designed to strengthen long-term impact. Up to €10,000 in flexible funding Support for grassroots climate justice and community-led initiatives Strengthening of feminist, Indigenous, and decolonial approaches Resources for storytelling, advocacy, and cultural knowledge preservation Support for healing, wellbeing, and intergenerational leadership Increased visibility for local movements across regional and global platforms This funding is designed to give communities control over how they define and implement their own solutions. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Youth-led organisations and collectives Women-led and gender-diverse groups Disability-inclusive grassroots initiatives Indigenous and community-rooted organisations Informal networks and unregistered groups (fiscal hosting available via Oxfam Pacific) Small organisations with limited access to funding Applicants must be based in eligible Pacific regions including Fiji, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu, Samoa, Tonga, Papua New Guinea, Kiribati, Tuvalu, Marshall Islands, Palau, Nauru, West Papua, Kanaky, and others listed in the call. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit a proposal outlining their organisation, theory of change, community engagement, and planned activities for a 6–12 month period. Applications must demonstrate alignment with feminist climate justice principles, including care, inclusion, and Indigenous knowledge systems. A detailed budget and supporting materials (such as documentation, media links, or past work) are required. Applications are accepted in English and French only. Deadline: 31 May 2026 at 11:59 PM Fiji Time Selection will be carried out by a Pacific-led advisory board of feminist climate justice practitioners. Learn more and apply on the official Oxfam Pacific website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Millennium Fellowship 2026 – Applications Open
summary: The United Nations Academic Impact in partnership with the Millennium Campus Network presents the Millennium Fellowship , a prestigious leadership development program for undergraduate students worldwide. This semester-long fellowship is designed to help young leaders turn their ideas into real social impact aligned with the ** United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). About the Fellowship The Millennium Fellowship takes place on your campus and supports students in developing and implementing projects that address global challenges. Fellows receive: World-class leadership training Access to a global network of changemakers Recognition from UN Academic Impact and MCN Support to implement a social impact project What Fellows Do Throughout the program, Fellows: Design and lead a project aligned with one or more SDGs Apply leadership and project management skills in real time Collaborate with peers and mentors Create measurable impact in their communities Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for: Undergraduate students from any discipline Young leaders passionate about social impact Students currently enrolled at a university Individuals ready to commit to a semester-long program Impact Snapshot (Class of 2025) 4,869 Fellows globally 2,550 projects launched 389,833 hours dedicated Over 1.2 million lives impacted Key Details Program Type: Fellowship / Leadership Duration: One semester (on-campus) Eligibility: Undergraduate students worldwide Final Deadline: 30 April 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters The Millennium Fellowship empowers students to move from ideas to action. It provides the structure, mentorship, and global platform needed to create meaningful, measurable change while building leadership skills. How to Apply Apply here. title: Short-Term Consulting Opportunities – World Bank (DECDI)
summary: The World Bank is hiring 9 consultants across 8 projects within its Development Impact Department (DECDI) . This is a great opportunity for professionals interested in contributing to global research and development impact initiatives through short-term consulting roles. About the Opportunity The Development Impact (DECDI) department focuses on evidence-based research to improve development outcomes worldwide. Selected consultants will support: Global research projects Data analysis and policy insights Program evaluation and impact measurement Development-focused research initiatives Applicants can also express interest in future consulting roles through the same application platform. Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for individuals who: Have experience in research, data analysis, or development economics Are interested in short-term, project-based consulting Have strong analytical and problem-solving skills Are passionate about global development and impact Key Details Number of Roles: 9 consultants across 8 projects Organization: World Bank – DECDI Application Deadline: 29 April 2026 Work Type: Short-term consulting Why This Opportunity Matters Working with the World Bank provides exposure to high-impact global research, collaboration with leading experts, and the chance to contribute to policy-relevant insights that shape development outcomes. How to Apply View project details and apply here. For more International Development opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Global Health Advisor Role – Ireland Mission to the UN (Geneva)
summary: The Permanent Mission of Ireland to the United Nations in Geneva is hiring a full-time Global Health Advisor to support its engagement in international health policy and multilateral diplomacy. This role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of global health, policy, and international relations within the United Nations system. About the Role The Global Health Advisor will support Ireland’s representation and engagement on global health issues in United Nations Office at Geneva . Key responsibilities may include: Providing strategic advice on global health policy Supporting negotiations and multilateral discussions Engaging with international stakeholders and partners Monitoring global health developments and trends Contributing to policy briefs, reports, and communications Who Should Apply This role is ideal for professionals who: Have experience in global health, public policy, or international development Possess strong analytical and research skills Have excellent communication and stakeholder engagement abilities Are interested in diplomacy and multilateral systems Can work in a fast-paced, international environment Key Details Position: Global Health Advisor Location: Geneva , Switzerland Start Date: May 2026 Duration: Initial contract of 2 years Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (16:30 Geneva time) Why This Opportunity Matters This role provides a platform to contribute to global health policy at one of the world’s key diplomatic hubs. It is an opportunity to influence international decision-making and collaborate with global actors working to improve health outcomes worldwide. How to Apply Learn more and apply here. title: United Nations Youth Delegate Programme Italy 2026/2027: Fully Funded Opportunity to Represent Italian Youth at the UN General Assembly
summary: The United Nations Youth Delegate Programme Italy (UNYDP Italy) 2026/2027 is now open for applications, offering a prestigious and impactful opportunity for young Italians to represent their country at the global stage. Organized by the Italian Society for International Organization (SIOI-UNA Italy) in collaboration with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) and under the patronage of the Italian Youth Agency (AIG), this programme selects two outstanding young leaders—one male and one female—to serve as official Youth Delegates to the United Nations. This highly competitive programme empowers youth voices in international policymaking and provides selected candidates with direct exposure to global diplomacy, particularly through participation in the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA). Overview of the UN Youth Delegate Programme Italy The UN Youth Delegate Programme Italy is a flagship initiative aimed at strengthening youth engagement in international governance and policy processes. Now in its 10th edition, the programme continues to build a strong pipeline of young leaders who contribute to global dialogue, sustainable development, and youth advocacy. Key Programme Details Programme Name: United Nations Youth Delegate Programme Italy 2026/2027 Organizers: SIOI-UNA Italy, MAECI, AIG Duration: September 2026 – September 2027 Number of Delegates: Two (one male and one female) Application Deadline: April 27, 2026, at 6:00 PM Why This Programme Matters The UN Youth Delegate Programme is more than just a leadership opportunity—it is a gateway into international diplomacy and policymaking. Selected delegates act as a bridge between Italian youth and global institutions, ensuring that youth perspectives are represented in key discussions related to sustainable development, peace, and international cooperation. Core Responsibilities of Selected Youth Delegates Successful candidates will take on significant responsibilities throughout their term, including: Participation in the United Nations General Assembly Engage in sessions of the 81st UN General Assembly Represent the perspectives and concerns of Italian youth National and International Engagement Organize and participate in meetings with students and youth organizations Attend international events and conferences Youth Advocacy and Communication Promote youth engagement in the implementation of the 2030 Agenda Use digital platforms and social media to amplify youth voices Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the UNYDP Italy 2026/2027, applicants must meet the following requirements: Basic Requirements Be between 20 and 27 years old at the time of application Hold Italian citizenship Enjoy full civil and political rights Be enrolled in a university programme or have completed one Language Requirements Excellent command of Italian (written and spoken) Strong proficiency in English (written and spoken) Knowledge of additional languages is an advantage Knowledge Requirements Candidates must demonstrate strong knowledge in: United Nations system and activities International law Current global affairs Italian foreign policy Structure of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Skills and Competencies Applicants should possess: Strong public speaking skills Teamwork and intercultural collaboration abilities Organizational and communication skills Digital communication and social media proficiency Preferential Criteria In cases of equal merit, preference will be given to candidates with: Participation in SIOI or MSOI activities Experience in youth organizations or volunteering Internships or roles in NGOs, UN agencies, or diplomatic missions International exposure (exchange programmes, volunteering abroad) Knowledge of graphic and digital tools Application Process Applicants must carefully follow all submission requirements to be considered. Required Documents CV in English (mandatory) Language certificates (optional) Additional relevant certifications (optional) Mandatory Video Submission Applicants must submit a video (maximum 120 seconds) in English covering: Personal introduction Motivation for applying Ideas on promoting youth engagement in the 2030 Agenda Submission Instructions Complete the online application form in English Submit required documents via the platform or email ( application@sioi.org ) Ensure all materials are submitted before the deadline Applications that are incomplete or submitted after the deadline will not be considered. Selection Process The selection process is rigorous and designed to identify the most qualified candidates. Stage 1: Application Screening Applications are reviewed in chronological order Stage 2: Online Interview Dates: May 19–21, 2026 Conducted via an online platform Assessment areas include: Knowledge of UN system and international affairs Italian and English proficiency Communication skills Stage 3: Final Selection Shortlisted candidates proceed to a final interview Final results announced by June 12, 2026 Two winners selected Training Phase Selected delegates will undergo mandatory training before assuming their roles. Training Details Timeline: June – July 2026 Format: Hybrid (online and/or in-person) Activities include: Mentorship from outgoing Youth Delegates Training sessions organized by SIOI Participants must be available to travel if required. Programme Benefits The programme provides extensive support and opportunities for personal and professional growth. Key Benefits Direct participation in UN General Assembly activities Exposure to international diplomacy and policymaking Networking with global leaders and youth representatives Development of leadership and advocacy skills Financial support for programme-related activities Important Dates to Remember Application Deadline: April 27, 2026 (6:00 PM) Interview Dates: May 19–21, 2026 Final Selection Announcement: June 12, 2026 Training Period: June – July 2026 Programme Start: September 2026 How to Apply Applicants must complete the Official Application Form and submit all required documents before the deadline. For inquiries: Email: application@sioi.org Website: www.sioi.org Click HERE to learn more about the United Nations Youth Delegate Programme Italy (UNYDP Italy) 2026/2027. Discover more other global Fellowship program on OFY HERE. title: Leverhulme Trust Early Career Fellowships 2026
summary: The Leverhulme Trust Early Career Fellowships support researchers at the early stages of their academic careers to undertake a significant piece of publishable research. These fellowships are hosted at UK universities with degree-awarding powers and are designed to help fellows transition into permanent academic roles. About the Fellowship This programme is aimed at researchers who already have a strong research track record but have not yet held a full-time permanent academic position. Fellowships are held at UK institutions and provide both financial and institutional support to develop independent research careers. Funding and Value The funding follows a joint support model: Year 1: The Trust covers 100% of salary costs up to £56,000 Years 2 and 3: The Trust covers 50% of salary costs up to £28,000 per year The host university covers the remaining balance Additional support includes: Up to £6,000 per year for research expenses Duration The fellowship lasts 3 years (full-time) Part-time options are available for health-related reasons, disability, or childcare commitments Eligibility Applicants must: Be early career researchers with a strong research record Not hold a full-time permanent academic position Secure a host institution at a UK university What the Fellowship Offers Full salary support (Year 1) and partial support (Years 2–3) Research funding to support project development Opportunity to build an independent academic profile Pathway to a permanent academic position Why This Opportunity Matters The Leverhulme Early Career Fellowship is one of the UK’s most respected research fellowships. It provides the time, funding, and institutional support needed to develop high-quality research and advance an academic career. Key Information Duration: 3 years Funding: Salary + up to £6,000/year research expenses Location: UK universities How to Apply Applications are submitted through the Leverhulme Trust Grants Management System. Applicants must coordinate with a host university before applying. Learn more For more Fellowship Opportunities , Visit OFY here. The post Leverhulme Trust Early Career Fellowships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
